$comment: Shared Governance Config — Single Source of Truth.
Cross-cutting definitions used by all validation scripts:
field registry, block composition, type registry, tag vocabulary, date format.
Doc type configs in types/ (adr.conf.json, evidence.conf.json) inherit from
this file via parent_config pointer and add domain-specific operational rules.
conf.schema.json       <- JSON Schema for this file
Governed by: ADR-26036 (Config File Location and Naming Conventions)

authors.description: Responsible party (owner, not necessarily the writer). List of {name, email} objects per MyST spec. Ecosystem minimum: name + email for every author.

description.description: Brief elevator pitch for progressive disclosure

birth.description: Creation date (set once on new file)

version.description: SemVer traceability — if date changed, version must change

id.description: Cross-reference identifier (numeric for ADRs, prefixed for evidence)

status.description: Lifecycle state (valid values defined per doc type config)

superseded_by.description: ADR ID that replaces this one

sources.description: List of source IDs that fed into this artifact

produces.description: List of ADR/artifact IDs this generates

model.description: AI model name or 'human' for meetings

extracted_into.description: Analysis ID after source extraction (null until extraction)

architecture.description: Technical design decisions — system structure, component boundaries, patterns

ci.description: Continuous integration — pipelines, hooks, automated checks, build steps

context_management.description: AI context engineering — token budgets, progressive disclosure, memory, RAG

development.description: Development methodology — code conventions, docstring contracts, AI-native workflows, language-agnostic practices

devops.description: Infrastructure and deployment — containers, databases, hosting, environments

documentation.description: Documentation standards — frontmatter, formatting, lifecycle, publishing

git.description: Git workflow — branching, commits, hooks, repository conventions

governance.description: Process and rules — ADR workflow, validation policies, naming conventions

hardware.description: Physical compute — GPU/CPU selection, VRAM/RAM constraints, device specs

model.description: AI models — selection criteria, taxonomy, capabilities, benchmarks

prompts.description: Prompt engineering — format, structure, token economics, injection resistance, delivery patterns

security.description: Security policies — API key management, access control, vulnerability scanning

testing.description: Test strategy — TDD, test quality standards, coverage, test infrastructure

workflow.description: Development workflow — task orchestration, agent collaboration, release process
